objectives: Full security audit of mx20022-runtime workspace
constraints: Financial transaction processing - high security bar
important_facts: Admin API uses bearer token auth with no cryptographic verification
Any non-empty bearer token is accepted as valid
TCP channel has no max frame size limit
No TLS on admin or inbound HTTP servers
Database URL leaked in health endpoint response
Internal error messages forwarded to HTTP clients
SQL queries use parameterized queries correctly
Crypto module uses SHA-256 for key derivation instead of proper KDF
risks_issues: CRITICAL: No real token validation - any string accepted as auth
CRITICAL: TCP frame size unbounded - OOM DoS vector
HIGH: Internal error strings leaked to clients
HIGH: Database URL leaked in health response
HIGH: No TLS on any listener

iteration.summary: Performed comprehensive security audit of 10 source files across the mx20022-runtime workspace. Identified 3 critical, 4 high, 4 medium, and 3 low severity findings. Key concerns: bearer token auth accepts any string without cryptographic verification, TCP channel allows unbounded frame allocation, internal error messages leak to HTTP clients, and database connection URLs are exposed via the health endpoint.
next_actions: Implement fixes for critical and high findings
